ucs.title: [LLM] Volatile Cedar (Lebanese Cedar) — IIS/Apache worker dropping ASPX/PHP/JSP web shell post-exploit
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Volatile Cedar initial-access → install chain: vulnerability scan of public-facing servers (T1595.002/003) followed by exploitation (T1190) of an Internet-facing web app, then deployment of a web shell (CheckPoint 2015 documented their custom 'Explosive' RAT loaders being staged via ASPX/PHP shells dropped by the web-server worker process itself). A web-server worker writing a server-script file into a web-served directory is the canonical artefact.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_hash) as file_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created (Filesystem.process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","php-cgi.exe","php-fpm*")) (Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.aspx","*.ashx","*.asmx","*.asp","*.php","*.php5","*.phtml","*.jsp","*.jspx","*.cfm")) (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\*","*\\wwwroot\\*","*/htdocs/*","*/www/*","*/webapps/*","*\\Tomcat*\\webapps\\*")) by host Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.user Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename host as dest | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Volatile Cedar — server-script file written by the web-server worker into a web root
let WebWorkers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","php-cgi.exe","php-fpm.exe"]);
let ShellExt = dynamic([".aspx",".ashx",".asmx",".asp",".php",".php5",".phtml",".jsp",".jspx",".cfm"]);
let WebRoots = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\wwwroot\", "/htdocs/", "/www/", "/webapps/", @"\Tomcat\webapps\"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebWorkers)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
   or InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("iusr","network service","apppool identity","www-data","apache","tomcat","nginx")
| extend FileNameLower = tolower(FileName), FolderPathLower = tolower(FolderPath)
| where ShellExt has_any (extract_all(@"\.[a-z0-9]+$", FileNameLower))
   or FileNameLower endswith ".aspx" or FileNameLower endswith ".ashx"
   or FileNameLower endswith ".asmx" or FileNameLower endswith ".asp"
   or FileNameLower endswith ".php"  or FileNameLower endswith ".php5"
   or FileNameLower endswith ".phtml" or FileNameLower endswith ".jsp"
   or FileNameLower endswith ".jspx" or FileNameLower endswith ".cfm"
| where FolderPathLower has_any (WebRoots)
// Pivot: did the same worker also pull tooling onto disk (Explosive RAT staging) within 1h?
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebWorkers)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".bin"
    | project DeviceId, ToolDropTime = Timestamp, ToolName = FileName, ToolPath = FolderPath, ToolSHA256 = SHA256
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(ToolDropTime) or (ToolDropTime between (Timestamp - 1h .. Timestamp + 1h))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          WebWorker = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ShellName = FileName, ShellPath = FolderPath, SHA256,
          ToolDropTime, ToolName, ToolPath, ToolSHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volatile Cedar — server-script file written by web-server worker into web root (ASIM)
let WebWorkers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","php-cgi.exe","php-fpm.exe"]);
let WebRoots = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\wwwroot\", "/htdocs/", "/www/", "/webapps/", @"\Tomcat\webapps\"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where ActingProcessName has_any (WebWorkers)
| extend FileNameLower = tolower(TargetFileName), FolderPathLower = tolower(TargetFilePath)
| where FileNameLower endswith ".aspx" or FileNameLower endswith ".ashx"
     or FileNameLower endswith ".asmx" or FileNameLower endswith ".asp"
     or FileNameLower endswith ".php"  or FileNameLower endswith ".php5"
     or FileNameLower endswith ".phtml" or FileNameLower endswith ".jsp"
     or FileNameLower endswith ".jspx" or FileNameLower endswith ".cfm"
| where FolderPathLower has_any (WebRoots)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName,
          TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volatile Cedar Web Shell Drop by IIS/Apache Worker
id: 7c2e0b1a-4cda-4d25-9a37-d24c8a9b1f01
status: experimental
description: Detects a web-server worker process writing a server-script file (ASPX/PHP/JSP/CFM) into a web-served directory — the install-stage artefact of Volatile Cedar / Lebanese Cedar (G0123) post-T1190 exploitation.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0123/
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2015/volatile-cedar/
  - https://www.clearskysec.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Lebanese-Cedar-APT.pdf
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g0123
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\php-cgi.exe'
      - '\php-fpm.exe'
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asmx'
      - '.asp'
      - '.php'
      - '.phtml'
      - '.jsp'
      - '.jspx'
      - '.cfm'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\'
      - '\wwwroot\'
      - '/htdocs/'
      - '/www/'
      - '/webapps/'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_ext and selection_path
falsepositives:
  - CMS auto-update (WordPress / Joomla) writing PHP files — allowlist by CMS-update path
  - Visual Studio publish to local IIS during dev
level: high
ucs.rationale: Volatile Cedar's documented MO (Check Point 2015, ClearSky 2021) is to scan and exploit Internet-facing web servers, then drop a web shell that subsequently stages the 'Explosive' RAT. Hunting on a web-server worker (w3wp/httpd/nginx/tomcat/php-fpm) creating a server-executable script file inside the web root is the highest-fidelity install-stage signal for this actor — legitimate web servers serv

ucs.title: [LLM] Volatile Cedar — IIS/Apache worker spawning Windows reconnaissance / cmd-shell child (post-web-shell hands-on)
ucs.description: Catches the actor's hands-on-keyboard step after their web shell lands: Volatile Cedar operators run Windows recon (whoami/net/systeminfo/tasklist) and download stagers via certutil/bitsadmin from inside their web shell. The parent will be the web-server worker process — a forensic tell that's nearly unique to web-server compromise.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe","php-cgi.exe","php-fpm*")) (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Volatile Cedar — recon / stager-download spawned by IIS/Apache worker
let WebWorkers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","php-cgi.exe","php-fpm.exe"]);
let ReconBins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe"]);
let ShellBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
let StagerBins = dynamic(["certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (WebWorkers)
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins) or FileName in~ (ShellBins) or FileName in~ (StagerBins)
| extend Bucket = case(
    FileName in~ (ReconBins), "recon",
    FileName in~ (StagerBins), "stager",
    "shell")
// suppression: legit web-app build/deploy launches CMD too — strip Visual Studio / msdeploy contexts
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine !has "msdeploy"
| where ProcessCommandLine !has "appcmd.exe"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          AccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          WebWorker = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          WebWorkerCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          Child = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          Bucket, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Volatile Cedar — recon / stager-download spawned by IIS/Apache worker (ASIM)
let WebWorkers = dynamic(["w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","nginx.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","php-cgi.exe","php-fpm.exe"]);
let ReconBins = dynamic(["whoami.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","hostname.exe","arp.exe","route.exe","netstat.exe"]);
let ShellBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe"]);
let StagerBins = dynamic(["certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ParentProcessName has_any (WebWorkers)
| extend ChildName = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildName in~ (ReconBins) or ChildName in~ (ShellBins) or ChildName in~ (StagerBins)
| where ParentProcessCommandLine !has "msdeploy"
| where TargetProcessCommandLine !has "appcmd.exe"
| extend Bucket = case(
    ChildName in~ (ReconBins), "recon",
    ChildName in~ (StagerBins), "stager",
    "shell")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine,
          TargetProcessName, TargetProcessCommandLine, Bucket
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Volatile Cedar Web Shell Hands-on Activity (Recon/Stager Spawned by Web Worker)
id: 9b1f7d2e-5e8a-4f5d-8b21-3f9d2e6c4a02
status: experimental
description: Detects classic post-web-shell hands-on activity — IIS/Apache/Tomcat/PHP-FPM worker process spawning Windows recon, cmd shell, or LOLBin downloader. Tightly mapped to Volatile Cedar (G0123) tradecraft post-T1190 / T1505.003.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0123/
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2015/volatile-cedar/
  - https://www.clearskysec.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/01/Lebanese-Cedar-APT.pdf
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.execution
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.g0123
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\httpd.exe'
      - '\nginx.exe'
      - '\tomcat.exe'
      - '\java.exe'
      - '\php-cgi.exe'
      - '\php-fpm.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\route.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
  filter_msdeploy:
    ParentCommandLine|contains: 'msdeploy'
  filter_appcmd:
    CommandLine|contains: 'appcmd.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not (filter_msdeploy or filter_appcmd)
falsepositives:
  - Web-app deployment pipelines that invoke appcmd or msdeploy
  - Custom CGI/PHP scripts that legitimately call out to system utilities
level: high
ucs.rationale: Volatile Cedar's tradecraft is server-side: scan → exploit web app → web shell → manual recon → stage Explosive RAT. The parent-of-cmd-shell predicate being a web-server worker is the single most differentiating tell for this actor as opposed to a phishing-borne intrusion. Bucketing into recon / shell / stager lets the analyst immediately read the kill-chain stage from the alert payload, and the m
